title: Queue Task Contract
description: Contract for Apple CLI bridge task handoff. Producer (OpenClaw agent) writes request files, consumer (host worker) writes state files. Two-directory model respects Docker mount boundaries: output/queue/ for requests, input/queue/ for state+results.

architecture.pattern: File-based task queue with lease semantics
architecture.producer: OpenClaw agent (Docker container) — writes request files via safe_fs_write_text
architecture.consumer: Host-side worker process — reads requests, executes via Apple CLI bridge, writes state files
architecture.note: No database, no message broker. Simplicity over throughput. Designed for <100 tasks/day.

requests.note: Agent creates request files here. Immutable once written.

state.note: Host creates and mutates state files here. Agent polls for results.

transitions.pending_to_leased: Worker picks up task, sets lease_owner + lease_until
transitions.leased_to_failed: Transient error, attempts < max_attempts. Worker waits visibility_timeout, then retries (back to pending internally).
transitions.leased_to_dead: Permanent error, OR attempts >= max_attempts. No more retries.
transitions.pending_to_dead: Pre-execution validation failure (invalid params, unsupported operation)

lifecycle.lease_expiry_recovery: If status=leased AND lease_until < now, treat as pending. The original worker is assumed crashed. Next scan picks it up.

lease_protocol.lease_owner: Worker identifier (hostname:pid or similar). Set when status transitions to leased.
lease_protocol.lease_until: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. lease_until = now + visibility_timeout_sec.
lease_protocol.visibility_timeout_sec: Default 300 (5 min). Configurable per task in request. Time a leased task is invisible to other workers / recovery scans.
lease_protocol.max_attempts: Default 3. Configurable per task in request. After max_attempts transient failures, task goes dead.
lease_protocol.note: v1 assumes single worker. Lease exists for crash recovery, not multi-worker concurrency.

transient.description: Temporary failure — retry will likely succeed

permanent.description: Will never succeed — do not retry

idempotency.task_id: 12-char hex. If idempotency_key is provided: SHA256(idempotency_key)[:12]. Otherwise: SHA256(random UUID)[:12].
idempotency.duplicate_detection: If state file already exists for task_id, worker skips. Agent should check for existing state before creating duplicate request.
idempotency.action_idempotency: read/list/search are naturally idempotent. create uses idempotency_key to prevent duplicates. Workers should check idempotency_key before executing create operations.

retention.behavior: After completed_at or dead_at + retain_days, worker deletes BOTH request and state files. Only touches {12-hex}.json files. Pending/leased tasks are never cleaned.

atomic_writes.why: Prevents agent from reading partially-written state files. rename() is atomic on macOS/Linux.
atomic_writes.note: Both producer and consumer MUST use atomic writes.

polling.agent_poll_interval: Agent checks input/queue/{task_id}.json every 5-10s after creating a request.
polling.worker_scan_interval: Worker scans output/queue/ every 5-10s for new requests.
polling.note: File-based polling is simple but adds latency. Acceptable for v1 (<100 tasks/day).

request_schema.description: Task request file created by the agent in workspace/output/queue/. Immutable once written.

task_id.description: 12-char hex identifier. Also the filename stem.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when agent created this request

priority.description: High-priority tasks are processed before normal in the scan order.

idempotency_key.description: Optional. If set, task_id = SHA256(idempotency_key)[:12]. Used for dedup on create operations.

integrity_hash.description: Optional (v1.1.0). SHA256 hex digest of canonical action JSON: json.dumps(action, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')). Integrity check, not authentication — no shared secret. Worker SHOULD verify if present, MUST NOT require.

max_attempts.description: Max retry attempts for transient errors before dead-letter.

visibility_timeout_sec.description: Seconds to wait between retries and for lease expiry.

state_schema.description: Task state file created and mutated by the host worker in workspace/input/queue/. Agent reads this to poll for results.

attempts.description: Number of execution attempts so far.

lease_owner.description: Worker identifier. Set when leased, cleared on release.

lease_until.description: Lease expiry. If now > lease_until and status=leased, lease has expired.

first_seen_at.description: When the worker first detected this request.

completed_at.description: Set when status transitions to done.

dead_at.description: Set when status transitions to dead.

result.description: Populated on status=done. Service-specific response data.

error.description: Populated on status=failed or status=dead. Last error encountered.

operation.description: Operation to perform. Available operations vary by service.

params.description: Service-specific parameters. Validated by the worker, not the schema.

data.description: Operation-specific result. Object for structured results, string for simple responses, array for list operations.

type.description: Transient errors are retried. Permanent errors go to dead-letter.

code.description: Machine-readable error code for retry classification.
